text:
Once upon a time in a town whose name nobody could spell properly, there lived a cat named Mittens.

Every morning, Mittens would wake up on the front porch with a fish in her mouth. She’d leap onto the birdfeeder, snack on it, and then leap back down.

One evening, as dusk settled and stars began to twinkle through the sleepy sky, Mittens heard a peculiar sound—nothing like her usual pitter-patter. Something fluttered by her tail.

Curiosity bristled. Mittens bounded toward the source. As she got nearer, her ears perked up. There before her was a tiny bee humming quietly, not threatening her at all.

In fact, Mittens quite liked bees.

The bee spoke in a gentle buzz. "Oh dear, there you are! I was just finishing my collection of honeypots."

"Why are they...?" Mittens inquired with wide ears.
